notes_md:
**Lọ lem** means *Cinderella* — the fairy-tale girl who is poor, treated badly, but later becomes lucky and loved.  

It’s a noun, usually used as a name, so you don’t need a classifier in most cases. You can use it metaphorically to describe a girl who looks plain or unlucky at first but later “transforms” beautifully or successfully.  

Vietnamese people also say **cô bé lọ lem** to emphasize the “poor, pitiful girl” image.  

Think of it as a symbol of a glow-up story — from unnoticed to shining.
notes:
Lọ lem means Cinderella — the fairy-tale girl who is poor, treated badly, but later becomes lucky and loved.  

It’s a noun, usually used as a name, so you don’t need a classifier in most cases. You can use it metaphorically to describe a girl who looks plain or unlucky at first but later “transforms” beautifully or successfully.  

Vietnamese people also say cô bé lọ lem to emphasize the “poor, pitiful girl” image.  

Think of it as a symbol of a glow-up story — from unnoticed to shining.
exampleSentences: Hồi nhỏ, em rất thích nghe truyện về lọ lem và hoàng tử.
Mỗi lần xem phim hoạt hình lọ lem, chị đều cảm thấy rất vui.
Con gái anh muốn mặc váy giống như lọ lem trong bữa tiệc sinh nhật.
exampleSentencesTranslated: When I was little, I really liked listening to stories about Cinderella and the prince.
Every time she watches the Cinderella cartoon, she feels very happy.
My daughter wants to wear a dress like Cinderella at her birthday party.
